message:
fix(web/sse): inherit ambient cx in SseRecvState so client disconnect reaches poll_recv

Three-pass audit of the web surface (crates/frankenterm-core/src/web.rs
+ crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/*.rs):

  1. unwrap/expect on handler hot paths: clean. The only `.unwrap` /
     `.expect` call sites in the prod zone of web.rs (pre-test boundary
     at line 346) are `unwrap_or` / `unwrap_or_else` / `unwrap_or_default`
     fallback patterns — no panicking unwraps reach the request path.

  2. missing input validation on POST bodies: clean at the crate seam.
     All list-style POST / query endpoints thread their body-size limit
     through `WebRuntimeLimits::max_request_body_bytes` (web.rs:77, 94)
     and list-result limits through `max_list_limit` (web.rs:75). The
     tuning_config defaults are enforced via the `resolve_runtime_limits`
     indirection; handlers.rs:208 and :317 consume those limits when
     shaping storage queries.

  3. cancel wiring in SSE streams: defect found and fixed.

`SseRecvState::new()` at sse.rs:199-204 built a fresh
`crate::cx::for_request()` on every SSE stream construction and stored
it in `self.cx`. Every subsequent `poll_recv(&self.cx, poll_cx)` on
the inner `mpsc::Receiver<SseEvent>` polled against that detached Cx
forever — the Cx has no parent chain, so a cancellation sent to the
handler's ambient Cx (from an HTTP client disconnect, a graceful
shutdown via `shutdown_with_cx`, or any upstream parent-scope cancel)
never reached the SSE poll. The stream kept its receiver and the
associated upstream sender chain alive until the Sender half dropped,
delaying cleanup past the point the client had already given up.

Fix: replace the bare `for_request()` with the established
inherit-or-fallback idiom used everywhere else in web/ (sse.rs:343,
:440, :680, handlers.rs:208, :317) and the tailer fix landed in
dfbf0a31:

    cx: crate::cx::Cx::current()
        .unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request),

`SseByteStream::new(rx) → SseRecvState::new()` is called synchronously
during handler setup, so `Cx::current()` resolves to the handler's
ambient Cx and the SSE stream's poll_recv now sees parent
cancellations within one scheduling tick.

Compile-verified: `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime,web` finishes
clean in 2m 13s at /tmp/ft-pane6-target.

files.patch:
@@ -198,8 +198,21 @@ struct SseRecvState {
 impl SseRecvState {
     fn new() -> Self {
         Self {
+            // Inherit the ambient handler Cx so a cancellation propagated
+            // from the web server's parent — an HTTP client disconnect, a
+            // graceful-shutdown signal from `shutdown_with_cx`, or a
+            // parent-scope cancel — reaches the inner `poll_recv` on this
+            // SSE stream. The earlier revision unconditionally called
+            // `crate::cx::for_request()`, which produces a detached
+            // request-scoped Cx with no parent chain; `poll_recv(&self.cx,
+            // …)` would then wait for events until the upstream Sender
+            // dropped, delaying cleanup of the mpsc receiver and its
+            // associated channels past the point where the HTTP client
+            // already gave up. Matches the inherit-or-fallback idiom at
+            // sse.rs:343 / :440 / :680, handlers.rs:208 / :317, and the
+            // tailer fix landed in dfbf0a31.
             #[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]
-            cx: crate::cx::for_request(),
+            cx: crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request),
         }
     }
